enrichment.short_description: AI-first B2B video platform for hosting webinars and virtual events, then repurposing recordings into clips, blogs, and social posts.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: Goldcast is an AI B2B video platform for webinars, virtual events, recording, and repurposing content into clips and social posts.

features.title: Digital Events
features.description: Host webinars and virtual events with a polished, Netflix-style presentation experience.

features.title: Recording Studio
features.description: Capture HD video locally with individual tracks and dynamic scene changes for podcasts and testimonials.

features.title: Content Lab
features.description: AI agents transform long-form event and recording footage into clips, blogs, and social posts automatically.

features.title: Video Hubs
features.description: Host on-demand video libraries with built-in engagement tracking.

features.title: AI Search
features.description: Makes video content searchable so viewers can find specific moments.

features.title: Agentic Video Editor
features.description: AI-powered editing tools assist with clip creation and brand polishing.

features.title: Analytics & Dashboards
features.description: Measures engagement and reports on webinar and content performance.

pros: AI-driven Content Lab repurposes long-form video into clips, blogs, and social posts automatically
Native integrations with major marketing and CRM tools including Marketo, HubSpot, Eloqua, and Salesforce
Combines event hosting, a recording studio, and on-demand video hubs in a single platform
Used by recognizable B2B brands such as Intercom, FullStory, Hootsuite, and Brandwatch
cons: No public pricing is listed; all plans require a demo or sales conversation
Founding year, headquarters, and company size are not published on the site
Custom plan features like SSO and multi-language support are gated behind enterprise-tier bundles

faqs.question: What is Goldcast used for?
faqs.answer: Goldcast is used for hosting B2B webinars and virtual events, recording video content, and automatically repurposing it into clips, blogs, and social posts.

faqs.question: Does Goldcast integrate with CRM or marketing automation tools?
faqs.answer: Yes, Goldcast has native integrations with Marketo, HubSpot, Eloqua, and Salesforce.

faqs.question: Is Goldcast pricing publicly available?
faqs.answer: No, Goldcast's pricing page outlines plan categories (Content Lab, Recording Studio, Digital Events, Custom) but requires contacting sales or requesting a demo for costs.

faqs.question: Does Goldcast use AI?
faqs.answer: Yes, Goldcast uses AI in its Content Lab for repurposing, an Agentic Video Editor, AI Highlight Reels, and AI Search.

use_cases.title: B2B webinars and virtual events
use_cases.description: Host demand-generation webinars and virtual summits with an engaging presentation format.
use_cases.audience: Demand generation and event marketing teams

use_cases.title: Video content repurposing
use_cases.description: Turn webinar and event recordings into clips, blog posts, and social content using the Content Lab.

use_cases.title: Podcast and testimonial recording
use_cases.description: Use the Recording Studio to capture HD video with individual tracks for podcasts and customer testimonials.

long_form_blocks.heading: What is Goldcast?
paragraphs: Goldcast is an AI-first B2B video content platform used to host webinars and virtual events, record video with a built-in studio, and repurpose that footage into clips, blogs, and social posts.

Its Content Lab feature uses AI agents to automatically generate multi-modal assets from long-form video, while Video Hubs let teams host on-demand libraries with engagement tracking. Goldcast integrates natively with Marketo, HubSpot, Eloqua, and Salesforce.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who Goldcast is for
paragraphs: Goldcast is built for demand generation, event marketing, and content marketing teams that run B2B webinars and virtual events and want to turn that video into ongoing content, rather than for general-purpose video conferencing.

enrichment.short_description: Bizzabo is an Event Experience OS for B2B conferences, offering registration, a mobile app, networking, and Klik SmartBadges.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: Bizzabo is an event management platform for B2B conferences with registration, a mobile app, networking, and onsite smart badges.

features.title: Event Experience OS
features.description: Core platform for registration, ticketing, contact management, and event website/agenda building.

features.title: Bizzy AI attendee copilot
features.description: An AI assistant built into the platform to support attendees during events.

features.title: Branded mobile event app
features.description: Mobile app with push notifications and real-time agenda access, customizable to the event brand.

features.title: Klik SmartBadges
features.description: Wearable contactless badges enabling interactive networking, lead capture, and passive attendee tracking with live analytics.

features.title: Attendee networking and matchmaking
features.description: Tools to connect attendees with relevant peers, speakers, and sponsors.

features.title: Video streaming and broadcasting
features.description: Supports streaming and broadcast capabilities for hybrid and virtual sessions.

features.title: Open API and integrations
features.description: Embedded integrations plus an open API connecting to 2,500+ core business systems.

features.title: Sponsor, exhibitor, and speaker management
features.description: Tools for managing sponsor lead capture, exhibitor logistics, and speaker portals.

pros: Recognized as a G2 Leader across multiple enterprise categories with 437+ reviews rated 4.5+ stars
Broad integration ecosystem of 2,500+ core business systems plus an open API
Combines registration, mobile app, networking, streaming, and onsite Klik SmartBadge hardware in one suite
Used by large, recognizable organizations including Amazon, HubSpot, Bloomberg, and Snowflake
cons: Base Event Experience OS plan starts at $17,999/year with a 3-user minimum, an enterprise-level commitment
Klik SmartBadge hardware and premium add-ons (virtual experience, advanced onsite software, sponsor/speaker portals) are priced separately via custom quote
Founding year, headquarters, and company size are not published on the pages reviewed

faqs.question: How much does Bizzabo cost?
faqs.answer: The core Event Experience Operating System plan starts at $499 per user/month billed annually, with a 3-user minimum, totaling $17,999/year. Klik SmartBadges and premium add-ons are custom-quoted.

faqs.question: Does Bizzabo have a mobile app?
faqs.answer: Yes, Bizzabo offers a branded mobile event app with push notifications and agenda access.

faqs.question: What is Bizzy?
faqs.answer: Bizzy is Bizzabo's AI attendee copilot, built into the platform to enhance the event experience.

faqs.question: Does Bizzabo support onsite or hybrid events?
faqs.answer: Yes, through Klik SmartBadges for onsite networking and lead capture, plus video streaming for hybrid and virtual sessions.

faqs.question: Does Bizzabo have an API?
faqs.answer: Yes, Bizzabo provides embedded integrations and an open API connecting to 2,500+ business systems.

use_cases.title: Large-scale B2B conferences
use_cases.description: Run registration, ticketing, agendas, and networking for major B2B conferences.

use_cases.title: Corporate and internal training events
use_cases.description: Manage internal company events and training sessions with the same Event Experience OS.

use_cases.title: Association and nonprofit events
use_cases.description: Support membership and fundraising events with registration and attendee engagement tools.

use_cases.title: Higher education events
use_cases.description: Run conferences and events for academic institutions.

features: Email campaigns and event website builder
Advanced registration flows and mobile app
Branded smart badges with interactive networking
Passive attendee tracking and live analytics

long_form_blocks.heading: What is Bizzabo?
paragraphs: Bizzabo is an event management platform Bizzabo calls an 'Event Experience Operating System,' built for B2B conferences and large-scale corporate events. It covers registration, ticketing, website and agenda creation, a mobile event app, attendee networking, and sponsor/exhibitor/speaker management.

The platform also includes Klik SmartBadges, wearable contactless devices for onsite networking and lead capture, an AI attendee copilot called Bizzy, and an open API connecting to 2,500+ business systems. Its base plan starts at $499 per user/month billed annually with a 3-user minimum.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who Bizzabo is for
paragraphs: Bizzabo is aimed at event marketing and corporate event teams running B2B conferences, internal training, association events, or higher education events that need registration, onsite hardware, and networking tools in one enterprise-priced platform.

enrichment.short_description: SpotMe is an enterprise event engagement platform, focused on life sciences and pharma, that syncs attendee data to CRM systems.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: SpotMe is an enterprise event platform for life sciences and pharma, with mobile apps, CRM sync, and compliance certifications.

features.title: Registration and check-in
features.description: Streamlined attendee registration and event check-in.

features.title: Mobile event app
features.description: iOS and Android apps with real-time agenda access for attendees.

features.title: Attendee networking and matchmaking
features.description: Connects attendees with relevant peers and contacts at events.

features.title: Interactive engagement tools
features.description: Polls, Q&A, gamification, and word clouds to drive session engagement.

features.title: Multi-stream agenda management
features.description: Manages complex agendas with multiple concurrent session streams.

features.title: CRM and MarTech integrations
features.description: Connects with Salesforce, HubSpot, Marketo, Pardot, Eloqua, Veeva, RainFocus, and Swoogo.

features.title: Post-event content distribution
features.description: Delivers on-demand recordings and content after events end.

features.title: Analytics
features.description: Tracks registration, session engagement, and content consumption data.

pros: Deep focus on life sciences and pharma events, including a Veeva integration for compliance-sensitive workflows
GDPR, SOC2 Type II, and ISO 27001 compliant
Named a Leader in The Forrester Wave: All-In-One Event Management Platforms, Q4 2024
Supports in-person, hybrid, virtual, webinar, and on-demand formats on one platform
Native iOS and Android mobile apps
cons: No public pricing; all four tiers (Starter, Business, Advanced, Enterprise) require contacting sales
Plans are capped by event count (e.g., 2 events on Starter, 5 on Business, 25 on Advanced), requiring an upgrade as usage grows
Production add-on services (onsite coordination, live production, interpretation) cost an additional $3,080-$7,700+

faqs.question: What is SpotMe used for?
faqs.answer: SpotMe is an enterprise event engagement platform used to run in-person, hybrid, virtual, and on-demand events, with a strong focus on life sciences and pharmaceutical companies.

faqs.question: Does SpotMe have a mobile app?
faqs.answer: Yes, SpotMe offers web and mobile apps for both iOS and Android with real-time agenda access.

faqs.question: What integrations does SpotMe support?
faqs.answer: SpotMe integrates with Salesforce, HubSpot, Marketo, Pardot, Eloqua, Veeva, RainFocus, and Swoogo.

faqs.question: Is SpotMe compliant with data regulations?
faqs.answer: Yes, SpotMe is GDPR, SOC2 Type II, and ISO 27001 compliant.

faqs.question: Does SpotMe publish its pricing?
faqs.answer: No, SpotMe offers four tiers (Starter, Business, Advanced, Enterprise) distinguished by event count and features, but all require contacting sales for exact pricing.

use_cases.title: Life sciences and pharma events
use_cases.description: Run compliance-sensitive conferences and roadshows with Veeva integration and certified data security.

use_cases.title: Hosted conferences and field marketing events
use_cases.description: Manage registration, agendas, and networking for hosted conferences and field marketing programs.
use_cases.audience: Professional services and enterprise event teams

use_cases.title: Hybrid and virtual events
use_cases.description: Deliver in-person, hybrid, virtual, webinar, and on-demand event formats from one platform.

long_form_blocks.heading: What is SpotMe?
paragraphs: SpotMe is an enterprise event engagement platform headquartered in Lausanne, Switzerland. It provides registration, check-in, mobile apps, networking, and multi-stream agenda tools for in-person, hybrid, virtual, and on-demand events, syncing attendee interaction data to CRM systems.

SpotMe integrates with Salesforce, HubSpot, Marketo, Pardot, Eloqua, Veeva, RainFocus, and Swoogo, and is GDPR, SOC2 Type II, and ISO 27001 compliant. It was named a Leader in The Forrester Wave: All-In-One Event Management Platforms, Q4 2024.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who SpotMe is for
paragraphs: SpotMe is built for enterprise organizers running hosted conferences, roadshows, and field marketing events, with particular strength in life sciences and pharmaceutical use cases that require compliance certifications and Veeva integration.

name: Hopin
skip_reason: hopin.com now displays 'Hopin is now RingCentral Events' — RingCentral acquired Hopin's Events and Session products and the site redirects/rebrands into RingCentral's product, with no pricing or standalone Hopin branding remaining. The original Hopin platform is discontinued as a standalone offering, so it does not qualify for enrichment under this name.

enrichment.short_description: All-in-one event management platform with an award-winning app for in-person, hybrid, and virtual events.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: Whova: event management platform with registration, an award-winning event app, exhibitor tools, and hybrid/virtual streaming.

features.title: Event App
features.description: A top-rated mobile event app that gives attendees personalized agendas, real-time updates, and networking tools, with the goal of helping attendees build more connections.

features.title: Registration & Ticketing
features.description: Flexible online registration and ticketing options for organizers to manage attendee sign-ups.

features.title: Event Management Tools
features.description: Tools for badges, check-in, surveys, and certificates to handle on-site and post-event logistics.

features.title: Abstract Management / Call for Speakers
features.description: Workflow for collecting and managing speaker submissions and abstracts.

features.title: Event Website Builder
features.description: A website builder with 20+ templates for creating an event's public-facing site.

features.title: Exhibitor & Sponsor Management
features.description: Tools for managing exhibitors and sponsors, including lead retrieval capabilities.

features.title: Hybrid & Virtual Events
features.description: Live streaming support for running hybrid and virtual event formats alongside in-person events.

features.title: MicroEvents
features.description: A feature set for managing smaller, sub-events (MicroEvents) within a larger event program.

pros: Covers the full event lifecycle in one platform: registration, app, on-site tools, and exhibitor/sponsor management
Mobile event app is positioned as award-winning and top-rated for networking
Recognized with a G2 'Global Top 50' award
Featured in Forbes, Inc., and San Diego Business Journal, indicating industry recognition
Offers dedicated tooling for both fully virtual and hybrid event formats, not just in-person
cons: No public pricing is listed; organizers must request a quote or book a demo to learn costs
Specific platform support (iOS/Android/web) and language support are not detailed on the marketing pages reviewed
No documented AI features on the pages reviewed
Free trial is only available by booking a 1:1 demo, not self-serve

faqs.question: Does Whova publish its pricing?
faqs.answer: No. Whova's pricing page directs visitors to request a custom quote based on event format, duration, expected attendees, and annual event frequency, or to book a demo.

faqs.question: Can I try Whova before buying?
faqs.answer: Whova's pricing page offers a '20 min 1:1 demo to get a trial,' rather than a self-serve free trial.

faqs.question: What types of events does Whova support?
faqs.answer: Whova is built for in-person, hybrid, and virtual events, including support for smaller MicroEvents within a larger event program.

faqs.question: What does the Whova event app do?
faqs.answer: It gives attendees personalized agendas, real-time updates, live polls, messaging, photo sharing, and live announcements, and is marketed as helping attendees build more connections.

faqs.question: Does Whova support exhibitors and sponsors?
faqs.answer: Yes, Whova includes exhibitor and sponsor management tools with lead retrieval capabilities.

use_cases.title: Conference and event organizers
use_cases.description: Manage registration, ticketing, badges, check-in, and surveys for a single event from one platform.
use_cases.audience: Event planners and organizers running in-person or hybrid conferences

use_cases.title: Hybrid and virtual event programs
use_cases.description: Run live-streamed sessions for remote attendees alongside an in-person event.
use_cases.audience: Organizations running hybrid or fully virtual events

use_cases.title: Exhibitor and sponsor-driven events
use_cases.description: Give exhibitors and sponsors dedicated management tools and lead retrieval within the event app.

use_cases.title: Multi-track or multi-event programs
use_cases.description: Use MicroEvents to manage smaller sub-events nested within a larger event program.
use_cases.audience: Organizers running multi-track conferences or event series

long_form_blocks.heading: What is Whova?
paragraphs: Whova is an all-in-one event management platform for in-person, hybrid, and virtual events, headquartered in San Diego, CA. It combines registration and ticketing, an attendee-facing mobile event app, on-site management tools (badges, check-in, surveys, certificates), an event website builder, and exhibitor/sponsor management with lead retrieval.

The platform has been recognized with a G2 'Global Top 50' award and has been covered by outlets including Forbes, Inc., and the San Diego Business Journal. Whova does not publish self-serve pricing; organizers get a custom quote based on event details or book a demo.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who Whova is for
paragraphs: Whova is aimed at event organizers and planners running conferences, trade shows, and multi-track events that need registration, an attendee app, and exhibitor/sponsor tools in one system, spanning in-person, hybrid, and virtual formats.

enrichment.short_description: AI-powered event management platform for registration, venue sourcing, mobile apps, and event ROI reporting.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: Cvent: AI-powered event management platform for registration, venue sourcing, mobile apps, check-in, and event ROI reporting.

features.title: Registration & Marketing
features.description: Tools for building event registration flows and running event marketing campaigns.

features.title: Venue Sourcing
features.description: Access to a network of 340,000+ venues and support for sourcing RFPs, with 300,000+ unique RFPs sourced annually across the platform.

features.title: Event Diagramming
features.description: Tools for creating floor plans and event space diagrams.

features.title: Speaker Management
features.description: Tools for managing speakers as part of event planning.

features.title: Mobile Event Apps
features.description: Mobile apps for attendees to access event information on-site.

features.title: Check-in & Badging
features.description: On-site check-in and badge printing tools.

features.title: Trade Show Lead Capture & Lead Retrieval
features.description: Tools for exhibitors to capture and retrieve leads at trade shows.

features.title: CventIQ (AI)
features.description: Cvent's AI layer, described as applying AI across the event program from initial venue search through final ROI reporting.

pros: Broad platform covering the full event lifecycle: sourcing, registration, on-site execution, and reporting
Large venue sourcing network (340,000+ venues) and high RFP volume (300,000+ sourced annually)
Used by 89% of the Fortune 100 and 34,000+ customers globally, per company-stated figures
25+ years of operating history in the event technology category (founded 1999)
Dedicated AI layer (CventIQ) built into the platform for venue search through ROI reporting
Committing $1B+ in product investment between July 2026 and July 2029, per company statements
cons: No public self-serve pricing; only a free trial for event registration and demo requests are offered
Platform breadth (many modules) may be more than smaller organizers need
Stated language support on the homepage is limited to English and Deutsch

faqs.question: Does Cvent publish its pricing?
faqs.answer: No public pricing is disclosed. Cvent offers a free event registration trial and demo requests instead of listed plan prices.

faqs.question: When was Cvent founded and where is it based?
faqs.answer: Cvent was founded in 1999 by Reggie Aggarwal and is headquartered at 1765 Greensboro Station Place, 7th Floor, Tysons Corner, VA 22102.

faqs.question: Does Cvent have AI features?
faqs.answer: Yes. Cvent's AI capabilities are branded CventIQ, which the company describes as applying AI across the event program from venue search to final ROI reporting.

faqs.question: How many venues and customers does Cvent's network include?
faqs.answer: Cvent states its ecosystem includes 340,000+ venues, 150,000+ active planners, and 34,000+ customers globally, with 8M+ events managed on the platform.

faqs.question: What does Cvent offer for trade shows and exhibitors?
faqs.answer: Cvent provides trade show lead capture and lead retrieval tools for exhibitors.

use_cases.title: Corporate event programs
use_cases.description: Manage registration, marketing, venue sourcing, and reporting for corporate events at scale.

use_cases.title: Venue and RFP sourcing
use_cases.description: Search and source venues through Cvent's network of 340,000+ venues and submit RFPs.

use_cases.title: Trade shows and exhibitions
use_cases.description: Run on-site check-in, badging, and exhibitor lead capture/retrieval for trade show events.

use_cases.title: Webinars and virtual experiences
use_cases.description: Run virtual experiences and webinars through Cvent's virtual experience platform.
use_cases.audience: Marketing and event professionals running virtual programs

long_form_blocks.heading: What is Cvent?
paragraphs: Cvent is an AI-powered event management platform founded in 1999 by Reggie Aggarwal and headquartered in Tysons Corner, VA. It covers registration and marketing, venue sourcing and RFPs, event diagramming, speaker management, mobile event apps, check-in and badging, trade show lead capture, virtual experiences, webinars, and event reporting.

The company states its platform has managed 8M+ events, serves 34,000+ customers globally, and is used by 89% of the Fortune 100. Its ecosystem includes 340,000+ venues and 150,000+ active planners, with 300,000+ unique RFPs sourced annually. Cvent's AI capabilities are branded CventIQ, applied from venue search through ROI reporting.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who Cvent is for
paragraphs: Cvent targets event, marketing, hospitality, and travel management professionals running corporate events, trade shows, and virtual/hybrid programs that need venue sourcing, registration, on-site execution, and ROI reporting in one platform.

enrichment.short_description: Event management platform for websites, registration, check-in, badge printing, mobile apps, and virtual events.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: EventMobi: event websites, registration, check-in, badge printing, and virtual/hybrid tools. Plans from $3,000/event or $8,900/year.

features.title: Event websites & registration
features.description: Build event websites and manage attendee registration flows.

features.title: Onsite check-in & badge printing
features.description: Check attendees in and print badges onsite, with hardware rental options available.

features.title: Event mobile app
features.description: A branded mobile app for attendees to navigate the event.

features.title: GoLive virtual event platform
features.description: Run virtual or hybrid sessions with streaming and video storage add-ons.

features.title: Exhibitor lead capture
features.description: Tools for exhibitors to capture and qualify leads onsite.

features.title: Gamification & networking
features.description: Appointment booking, live polls, and gamification to drive engagement.

features.title: MobiAI / AI Reg Concierge
features.description: AI-branded features including an AI-powered registration concierge.

features.title: 1000+ integrations
features.description: Standard integrations plus a free API and Zapier connection.

pros: Publishes actual starting prices ($3,000/event or $8,900/year) rather than requiring a quote for baseline plans
Supports 27 languages
SOC 2 Type II, ISO 27001, GDPR, and PCI-DSS Level 1 compliant
Free trial available after a personalized demo
1000+ integrations including a free API and Zapier connection
cons: No self-serve free plan; a demo is required to unlock the free trial
Additional costs apply for badge hardware rental, streaming/video storage, and custom integrations
Full year-round or multi-event pricing still requires a custom quote
Founding year and company employee count are not disclosed on the site

faqs.question: Does EventMobi have a free plan?
faqs.answer: No. EventMobi does not offer a free plan, but a free trial is available after a personalized demo and platform walkthrough.

faqs.question: How much does EventMobi cost?
faqs.answer: Per Event pricing starts at $3,000 per event; Per Year pricing for unlimited events starts at $8,900 annually.

faqs.question: Does EventMobi support multiple languages?
faqs.answer: Yes, EventMobi's platform supports 27 languages.

faqs.question: Is EventMobi compliant with data security standards?
faqs.answer: Yes, EventMobi is SOC 2 Type II certified, ISO 27001 infrastructure, GDPR compliant, and PCI-DSS Level 1 for payments.

faqs.question: Does EventMobi support virtual or hybrid events?
faqs.answer: Yes, via its GoLive virtual event platform, with streaming and video storage available as add-ons starting at $500 per event.

use_cases.title: Corporate conferences
use_cases.description: End-to-end event website, registration, check-in, and app tools for corporate events.

use_cases.title: Association and nonprofit events
use_cases.description: Special pricing is available for non-profits and multi-year contracts.

use_cases.title: Trade shows and exhibitor events
use_cases.description: Exhibitor lead capture and badge printing for trade show organizers.

features: Phone, email, and live chat support
Unlimited events on the end-to-end platform

long_form_blocks.heading: What is EventMobi?
paragraphs: EventMobi is an event management platform that combines event websites, registration, onsite check-in, badge printing, a mobile event app, and a virtual/hybrid platform called GoLive into one system.

The platform publishes baseline starting prices — $3,000 per event or $8,900 per year for unlimited events — rather than requiring a sales quote for every tier, and includes MobiAI-branded features such as an AI Reg Concierge.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who EventMobi is for
paragraphs: EventMobi is aimed at associations, nonprofits, corporations, and event agencies running conferences, trade shows, and hybrid events, with special pricing offered for non-profits and multi-year contracts.

enrichment.short_description: AI-powered event platform for registration, matchmaking, exhibitor management, and analytics at trade shows.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: Swapcard: AI matchmaking, registration, exhibitor tools, and analytics for trade shows and conferences. Starter, Professional, and Enterprise plans.

features.title: Flexible ticketing & registration
features.description: Configure registration flows and flexible ticket pricing.

features.title: Sherlock AI matchmaking
features.description: AI-powered attendee matching and personalized recommendations.

features.title: Exhibitor management portal & marketplace
features.description: Tools for exhibitors to manage presence and participate in a marketplace.

features.title: Lead capture & qualification
features.description: Capture and qualify leads for exhibitors and sponsors.

features.title: Mobile app
features.description: iOS and Android event app reported to reach 70-80% attendee adoption.

features.title: Analytics & reporting
features.description: Dashboards covering event and attendee engagement data.

features.title: Onsite check-in & badge printing
features.description: Check attendees in and print badges onsite.

features.title: 30+ native integrations
features.description: Native integrations including Salesforce and HubSpot.

pros: Sherlock AI provides AI-driven matchmaking and personalized attendee recommendations
Native iOS and Android apps with reported 70-80% attendee adoption
30+ native integrations including Salesforce and HubSpot
SOC 2 Type 2, ISO 27001, and GDPR compliance across all plans
Starter plan includes a free trial
cons: No public pricing for any plan; Starter, Professional, and Enterprise all require custom quotes
Annual billing only, with no monthly option
Downgrades are only permitted at term renewal
Attendee/exhibitor caps (Starter: 200/year, Professional: 1,000/year) may require paid add-on packages

faqs.question: Does Swapcard offer a free trial?
faqs.answer: Yes, the Starter plan offers a free trial; Professional and Enterprise require scheduling a demo.

faqs.question: What plans does Swapcard offer?
faqs.answer: Starter (up to 200 attendees/year), Professional (up to 1,000 attendees/year), and Enterprise (unlimited attendees).

faqs.question: Does Swapcard have mobile apps?
faqs.answer: Yes, native iOS and Android apps are available.

faqs.question: What AI features does Swapcard include?
faqs.answer: Sherlock AI powers matchmaking, personalized recommendations, AI agents for attendee support, and predictive analytics.

faqs.question: Is Swapcard billed monthly or annually?
faqs.answer: Swapcard uses annual subscriptions only, with Net 30 days payment terms.

use_cases.title: Trade show organizers
use_cases.description: Exhibitor marketplace, lead capture, and hosted buyer program coordination.

use_cases.title: Association & conference events
use_cases.description: Registration, session management, and AI matchmaking for conferences.

use_cases.title: Media and nonprofit events
use_cases.description: Analytics and engagement tools for media companies and nonprofits.

long_form_blocks.heading: What is Swapcard?
paragraphs: Swapcard is an event management platform built around AI-powered matchmaking (branded 'Sherlock AI'), covering registration, exhibitor management, lead capture, and analytics for trade shows, conferences, and associations.

It offers three annually billed tiers — Starter (up to 200 attendees/year), Professional (up to 1,000 attendees/year), and Enterprise (unlimited attendees) — with pricing set through a custom quote combining a license fee and attendee/exhibitor volume.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who Swapcard is for
paragraphs: Swapcard targets trade show organizers, associations, conference organizers, media companies, and nonprofits that need exhibitor marketplaces, lead qualification, and AI-driven attendee matchmaking.

enrichment.short_description: AI-driven event networking platform with matchmaking, meeting scheduling, and white-labeled event apps.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: Brella: AI-driven matchmaking, meeting scheduling, and a white-labeled event app for conferences and tradeshows.

features.description: A customizable, white-labeled app for virtual, physical, and hybrid events.

features.title: AI-driven matchmaking
features.description: Intent-based, customizable matchmaking highlighting attendee interests, aimed at high meeting acceptance rates.

features.title: Lead scanning & meeting ratings
features.description: Scan leads and rate meetings during the event.

features.title: Group & managed meetings
features.description: Schedule group and managed meetings with custom locations.

features.title: Sponsor booths & matchmaking
features.description: Sponsor booth tools with matchmaking for exhibitors.

features.title: 500+ integrations & public API
features.description: A wide selection of native integrations plus a public API and managed integration services.

features.title: Flexible event scheduling
features.description: Multiple layout options for scheduling sessions and meetings.

features.title: Analytics
features.description: Reporting on attendance, engagement, and market trends.

pros: AI-driven, intent-based matchmaking that highlights attendee interests
Native iOS and Android apps plus a web app and embeddable widgets
500+ integration options with a public API
Reports a 9.5/10 NPS score for its service team
Used by known event organizers including Bits & Pretzels, Financial Times, Informa Markets, RX, Forbes, and SXSW Sydney
cons: No public pricing; requires contacting sales ('Talk to an event expert')
No published free trial or free plan
Founding year, headquarters, and company size are not disclosed on the site

faqs.question: Does Brella publish pricing?
faqs.answer: No. Brella directs prospects to talk to an event expert or contact sales for a custom quote.

faqs.question: Does Brella use AI?
faqs.answer: Yes, Brella uses intent-based, customizable AI matchmaking and AI-powered prospecting.

faqs.question: What platforms does Brella support?
faqs.answer: Native iOS and Android apps, a web app, embeddable widgets, and admin/sponsor portals.

faqs.question: How many integrations does Brella offer?
faqs.answer: Brella offers 500+ integration options plus a public API.

faqs.question: Who uses Brella?
faqs.answer: Associations, corporations, conferences, tradeshows, academic events, and internal events; listed clients include Financial Times, Forbes, and SXSW Sydney.

long_form_blocks.heading: What is Brella?
paragraphs: Brella is an event platform focused on networking and matchmaking, offering a white-labeled event app with AI-driven, intent-based matchmaking designed to increase meeting acceptance rates.

It provides group and managed meeting scheduling, lead scanning, sponsor booth matchmaking, 500+ integrations plus a public API, and analytics on attendance, engagement, and market trends. Pricing is not published and requires contacting sales.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who Brella is for
paragraphs: Brella is built for associations, corporations, conferences, tradeshows, academic events, and internal events that prioritize attendee networking and AI-driven matchmaking.

long_form_blocks.heading: What is Grip?
paragraphs: Grip is an AI-powered event technology platform, founded in 2016 and headquartered in London, built around automated matchmaking and pre-scheduled meetings for commercial events.

The platform combines an AI matchmaking engine, an AI Assistant for participant queries, onsite solutions like smart badges and entrance scanning, and lead retrieval tools, and states it has powered 5,000+ events with 15 million+ participants and 2 million+ meetings booked.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who Grip is for
paragraphs: Grip serves event professionals running conferences, trade shows, hosted buyer events, annual meetings, and confex events who want automated, data-driven attendee matchmaking.
